name: Your Application
description: OIDC client with hardened session settings
_publicClient_why: WHY: Use confidential client (publicClient=false). Confidential clients authenticate with a client secret. Public clients (SPAs) cannot keep secrets — if you must use public client, require PKCE.
_standardFlowEnabled_why: WHY: Authorization Code flow with PKCE. The only secure flow for user-facing applications. Never use implicit flow (deprecated in OAuth 2.1).
_implicitFlowEnabled_why: WHY: Implicit flow is DISABLED. Tokens returned in URL fragments are vulnerable to referrer leakage and browser history exposure (SC-23).
_directAccessGrantsEnabled_why: WHY: Resource Owner Password Credentials (ROPC) grant is DISABLED. ROPC bypasses MFA and is the OAuth equivalent of basic auth. Never enable in production.
_serviceAccountsEnabled_why: WHY: No service account needed for user-facing apps. Only enable for server-to-server flows (client credentials grant).
_consentRequired_why: WHY: Require user consent for scope grants. Users must acknowledge what data the application accesses. Supports privacy and IA-8 controls for non-org users.
_pkce_comment: PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) — RFC 7636

attributes._pkce_why: WHY: PKCE with S256 (SHA-256) prevents authorization code interception attacks. Required for all public clients; recommended for confidential clients too. S256 > plain (plain is vulnerable to MITM).
attributes._access_token_lifespan_why: WHY: Access tokens expire in 5 minutes. Short-lived tokens limit blast radius if a token is intercepted or leaked. Client must refresh frequently.
attributes._client_session_idle_why: WHY: Client-specific idle timeout of 30 minutes. Overrides realm default for this client. Matches AC-12 session termination requirement.
attributes._client_session_max_why: WHY: Maximum 10-hour session for this client. User must re-authenticate after 10 hours regardless of activity.
attributes._refresh_tokens_why: WHY: Enable refresh tokens. Required for maintaining sessions beyond access token lifetime without forcing re-login every 5 minutes.
attributes._offline_session_idle_why: WHY: Offline sessions disabled. Offline tokens persist indefinitely and cannot be revoked via IdP session termination — avoid unless required.

_redirect_comment: Redirect URI whitelist — SC-23 (prevent open redirect token theft)
_redirectUris_why: WHY: Explicit redirect URI whitelist. Wildcards at path level only — never wildcard the domain. Open redirect URIs allow attackers to steal authorization codes by redirecting to attacker-controlled domains.
_webOrigins_why: WHY: CORS allowed origins explicitly listed. Prevents cross-origin token requests from attacker domains. Never use '+' (all allowed redirect URIs) in production.
_defaultClientScopes_why: WHY: Minimal scopes. Do not include scopes application does not use. Principle of least privilege applied to OAuth scope.
_optionalClientScopes_why: WHY: No optional scopes by default. Add only what is required per application.
_fullScopeAllowed_why: WHY: Full scope DISABLED. Prevents token from including all realm roles. Token should only contain roles relevant to this client.
_token_comment: Token settings — SC-23 (session authenticity via signed tokens)

protocolMappers.name: jti-mapper
protocolMappers._why: WHY: JTI (JWT ID) claim enables per-token revocation tracking. Without JTI, you cannot revoke individual tokens, only expire by time. Required for NIST AC-12 session termination.
